post.description:
Create a new organization with an administrative user. If no specific roles are sent for the users, they will be granted the role ORG_OWNER.

 Required permission:
  - `org.create`

Search for Organizations. By default, we will return all organization of the instance that you have permission to read.
 Make sure to include a limit and sorting for pagination.

 Required permission:
  - `org.read`

Change the name of the organization.

 Required permission:
  - `org.write`

parameters.name: organization_id
parameters.description: OrganizationID is the unique identifier of the organization to be updated.

name.title: name
name.description:
Name is the new name for the organization to be set.
 Note that since the name is used to generate the organization's default domain,
 changing the name will also change the domain.
 Additionally, if the domain is used as suffix for user logins,
 their login names will also change accordingly.
 It will not affect any Organization Domains added to the organization.

delete.description:
Deletes the organization and all its resources (Users, Projects, Grants to and from the org). Users of this organization will not be able to log in.

 Required permission:
  - `org.delete`

parameters.name: organization_id
parameters.description: OrganizationID is the unique identifier of the organization to be deleted.

post.description:
Set the state of my organization to active. The state of the organization has to be deactivated to perform the request. Users of this organization will be able to log in again.

 Required permission:
  - `org.write`

parameters.name: organization_id
parameters.description: OrganizationID is the unique identifier of the organization to be activated.

post.description:
Sets the state of my organization to deactivated. Users of this organization will not be able to log in.

 Required permission:
  - `org.write`

parameters.name: organization_id
parameters.description: OrganizationID is the unique identifier of the organization to be deactivated.

post.description:
Add a new domain to an organization. The domains are used to identify to which organization a user belongs.

 Required permission:
  - `org.write`

parameters.name: organization_id
parameters.description: OrganizationID is the unique identifier of the organization to which the domain is to be added.

domain.title: domain
domain.description:
Domain is the full qualified domain name to be added to the organization.
 Note that the domain has to be unique across the instance.
 Depending on the settings, you might have to verify the domain before it can be used.

delete.description:
Delete a new domain from an organization. The domains are used to identify to which organization a user belongs. If the uses use the domain for login, this will not be possible afterwards. They have to use another domain instead.

 Required permission:
  - `org.write`

parameters.name: organization_id
parameters.description: OrganizationID is the unique identifier of the organization from which the domain is to be deleted.

parameters.name: domain
parameters.description:
Domain is the full qualified domain name to be deleted from the organization.
 Note that if the domain is used as suffix for user logins,
 those users will not be able to log in anymore. They have to use another domain instead.
 Also if the domain was used for domain discovery,
 users will not be able to find the organization by the domain anymore.

post.description:
Returns the list of registered domains of an organization. The domains are used to identify to which organization a user belongs.

 Required permission:
  - `org.read`

parameters.name: organization_id
parameters.description: OrganizationID is the unique identifier of the organization from which the domains are to be listed.

filters.title: filters
filters.description: Filters define the criteria to query for.

sortingColumn.title: sorting_column
sortingColumn.description:
SortingColumn is the field the result is sorted by.
 Beware that if you change this, your result pagination might be inconsistent.

post.description:
Generate a new file to be able to verify your domain with DNS or HTTP challenge.

 Required permission:
  - `org.write`

parameters.name: organization_id
parameters.description: OrganizationID is the unique identifier of the organization for which the domain validation is to be generated.

domain.title: domain
domain.description: Domain is the full qualified domain name for which the validation is to be generated.

type.title: type
type.description:
Type is the domain validation type to be generated.
 Depending on the type, you will have to add a DNS record or a file to your webserver.
 Make sure that the challenge is reachable via the chosen method.
 The validation has to be done within one hour, otherwise the token will expire and you will have to generate a new one.
 After you have added the record or the file, you can verify the domain via the VerifyOrganizationDomain endpoint.
 You can check the status of the domain via the ListOrganizationDomains endpoint.
 The domain will be marked as verified after a successful verification.

post.description:
Make sure you have added the required verification to your domain, depending on the method you have chosen (HTTP or DNS challenge). Zitadel will check it and set the domain as verified if it was successful. A verify domain has to be unique.

 Required permission:
  - `org.write`

parameters.name: organization_id
parameters.description: Organization ID is the unique identifier of the organization whose domain is to be verified.

domain.title: domain
domain.description: Domain is the full qualified domain name to be verified.

post.description:
Sets a list of key value pairs. Existing metadata entries with matching keys are overwritten. Existing metadata entries without matching keys are untouched. To remove existing metadata entries, pass an empty metadata value or use [DeleteOrganizationMetadata](/docs/reference/api/org/zitadel.org.v2.OrganizationService.DeleteOrganizationMetadata). If no metadata entry exists for a given key, passing an empty value for that key has no effect (no-op). For HTTP requests, make sure the bytes array value is base64 encoded.

 Required permission:
  - `org.write`

parameters.name: organization_id
parameters.description: Organization ID is the unique identifier of the organization whose metadata is to be set.

metadata.title: metadata
metadata.description: Metadata is a list of metadata entries to set.

delete.description:
Delete metadata objects from an organization with a specific key.

 Required permission:
  - `org.write`

parameters.name: organization_id
parameters.description: Organization ID is the unique identifier of the organization whose metadata is to be deleted.

parameters.name: keys
parameters.description: Keys are the organization metadata entries to be deleted by their key.

post.description:
List metadata of an organization filtered by query.

 Required permission:
  - `org.read`

parameters.name: organization_id
parameters.description: Organization ID is the unique identifier of the organization whose metadata is to be listed.

filters.title: filters
filters.description: Filters define the criteria to query the metadata for.

offset.title: offset
offset.description: Starting point for retrieval, in combination of offset used to query a set list of objects.

limit.title: limit
limit.description:
limit is the maximum amount of objects returned. The default is set to 100
 with a maximum of 1000 in the runtime configuration.
 If the limit exceeds the maximum configured Zitadel will throw an error.
 If no limit is present the default is taken.

asc.title: asc
asc.description:
Asc is the sorting order. If true the list is sorted ascending, if false
 the list is sorted descending. The default is descending.

totalResult.title: total_result
totalResult.description: Absolute number of objects matching the query, regardless of applied limit.

appliedLimit.title: applied_limit
appliedLimit.description: Applied limit from query, defines maximum amount of objects per request, to compare if all objects are returned.

sequence.title: sequence
sequence.description:
sequence represents the order of events. It's always counting

 on read: the sequence of the last event reduced by the projection

 on manipulation: the timestamp of the event(s) added by the manipulation

changeDate.title: change_date
changeDate.description:
change_date is the timestamp when the object was changed

 on read: the timestamp of the last event reduced by the projection

 on manipulation: the timestamp of the event(s) added by the manipulation

resourceOwner.title: resource_owner
resourceOwner.description: resource_owner is the organization or instance_id an object belongs to

creationDate.title: creation_date
creationDate.description: creation_date is the timestamp where the first operation on the object was made

organizationId.title: organization_id
organizationId.description: OrganizationID is the unique identifier of the organization to be activated.

changeDate.title: change_date
changeDate.description: ChangeDate is the timestamp of the activation of the organization.

organizationId.title: organization_id
organizationId.description: OrganizationID is the unique identifier of the organization to which the domain is to be added.

domain.title: domain
domain.description:
Domain is the full qualified domain name to be added to the organization.
 Note that the domain has to be unique across the instance.
 Depending on the settings, you might have to verify the domain before it can be used.

creationDate.title: creation_date
creationDate.description: CreationDate is the timestamp when the Organization Domain was created.

name.title: name
name.description:
Name is the unique name of the organization to be created.
 This must be unique across the instance.

admins.title: admins
admins.description:
Specify users to be assigned as organization admins.
 If no users are specified here, the organization will be created without any admin users.
 The organization can still be managed by any instance administrator.
 If no roles are specified for a user, they will be assigned the role ORG_OWNER.

organizationId.title: organization_id
organizationId.description:
OrganizationID is the unique identifier of the organization. This field is optional.
 If omitted, the system will generate one,
 which is the recommended way. The generated ID will be returned in the response.

orgId.title: org_id
orgId.description:
Optionally, set a unique id for the organization. If omitted, the system will generate one,
 which is the recommended way. The generated ID will be returned in the response.

 Deprecated: use 'organization_id' field instead.
 If both org_id and organization_id are set, organization_id will take precedence.

roles.title: roles
roles.description: specify Org Member Roles for the provided user (default is ORG_OWNER if roles are empty)

organizationId.title: organization_id
organizationId.description: OrganizationID is the unique identifier of the organization to be deactivated.

changeDate.title: change_date
changeDate.description: ChangeDate is the timestamp of the deactivation of the organization.

organizationId.title: organization_id
organizationId.description: OrganizationID is the unique identifier of the organization from which the domain is to be deleted.

domain.title: domain
domain.description:
Domain is the full qualified domain name to be deleted from the organization.
 Note that if the domain is used as suffix for user logins,
 those users will not be able to log in anymore. They have to use another domain instead.
 Also if the domain was used for domain discovery,
 users will not be able to find the organization by the domain anymore.

deletionDate.title: deletion_date
deletionDate.description: DeletionDate is the timestamp of the deletion of the Organization Domain.

organizationId.title: organization_id
organizationId.description: Organization ID is the unique identifier of the organization whose metadata is to be deleted.

keys.title: keys
keys.description: Keys are the organization metadata entries to be deleted by their key.

deletionDate.title: deletion_date
deletionDate.description: DeletionDate is the timestamp of the deletion of the organization metadata.

organizationId.title: organization_id
organizationId.description: OrganizationID is the unique identifier of the organization to be deleted.

deletionDate.title: deletion_date
deletionDate.description: DeletionDate is the timestamp of the deletion of the organization.

organizationId.title: organization_id
organizationId.description: OrganizationID is the unique identifier of the organization the domain belongs to.

domain.title: domain
domain.description: Domain is the fully qualified domain name.

isVerified.title: is_verified
isVerified.description: IsVerified is a boolean flag indicating if the domain has been verified.

isPrimary.title: is_primary
isPrimary.description: IsPrimary is a boolean flag indicating if the domain is the Organization Domain of the organization.

validationType.title: validation_type
validationType.description: ValidationType indicates the protocol used to validate the domain ownership.

domainFilter.title: domain_filter
domainFilter.description: Filter Organization Domains by their domain name.

organizationId.title: organization_id
organizationId.description: OrganizationID is the unique identifier of the organization for which the domain validation is to be generated.

domain.title: domain
domain.description: Domain is the full qualified domain name for which the validation is to be generated.

type.title: type
type.description:
Type is the domain validation type to be generated.
 Depending on the type, you will have to add a DNS record or a file to your webserver.
 Make sure that the challenge is reachable via the chosen method.
 The validation has to be done within one hour, otherwise the token will expire and you will have to generate a new one.
 After you have added the record or the file, you can verify the domain via the VerifyOrganizationDomain endpoint.
 You can check the status of the domain via the ListOrganizationDomains endpoint.
 The domain will be marked as verified after a successful verification.

token.title: token
token.description:
Token is a verification token that needs to be added to the DNS records or as a file to the webserver.
 Zitadel will check for this token to verify the domain.

url.title: url
url.description: URL is the location where the token needs to be placed for HTTP challenge.

organizationId.title: organization_id
organizationId.description: OrganizationID is the unique identifier of the organization from which the domains are to be listed.

filters.title: filters
filters.description: Filters define the criteria to query for.

sortingColumn.title: sorting_column
sortingColumn.description:
SortingColumn is the field the result is sorted by.
 Beware that if you change this, your result pagination might be inconsistent.

pagination.title: pagination
pagination.description: Pagination of the organizations domain results.

domains.title: domains
domains.description: Domains is a list of fully qualified domain names registered to the organization matching the query.

organizationId.title: organization_id
organizationId.description: Organization ID is the unique identifier of the organization whose metadata is to be listed.

filters.title: filters
filters.description: Filters define the criteria to query the metadata for.

pagination.title: pagination
pagination.description: Pagination of the Organizations metadata results.

metadata.title: metadata
metadata.description: Metadata is a list of organization metadata that matched the query.

sortingColumn.title: sorting_column
sortingColumn.description: the field the result is sorted

queries.title: queries
queries.description: criteria the client is looking for

sortingColumn.title: sorting_column
sortingColumn.description: The sorting columns the result is sorted.

result.title: result
result.description: The Result is a list of organizations matching the query.

key.title: key
key.description: Key is identifier of the metadata entry.

value.title: value
value.description: Value in the metadata key/value pair. On updates, an empty value will delete the metadata entry.

id.title: id
id.description: ID is the unique identifier of the organization.

details.title: details
details.description: Details about the organizations' creation and change date.

state.title: state
state.description: Current state of the organization, for example active, inactive or deleted.

primaryDomain.title: primary_domain
primaryDomain.description: Organization Domain used in the organization.

domain.title: domain
domain.description: Domain used in organization, not necessary primary domain.

method.title: method
method.description: Defines which text equality method is used.

method.title: method
method.description: Defines which text equality method is used.

organizationId.title: organization_id
organizationId.description: Organization ID is the unique identifier of the organization whose metadata is to be set.

metadata.title: metadata
metadata.description: Metadata is a list of metadata entries to set.

setDate.title: set_date
setDate.description: The timestamp of the update of the organization metadata.

organizationId.title: organization_id
organizationId.description: OrganizationID is the unique identifier of the organization to be updated.

name.title: name
name.description:
Name is the new name for the organization to be set.
 Note that since the name is used to generate the organization's default domain,
 changing the name will also change the domain.
 Additionally, if the domain is used as suffix for user logins,
 their login names will also change accordingly.
 It will not affect any Organization Domains added to the organization.

changeDate.title: change_date
changeDate.description: ChangeDate is the timestamp of the update to the organization.

organizationId.title: organization_id
organizationId.description: Organization ID is the unique identifier of the organization whose domain is to be verified.

domain.title: domain
domain.description: Domain is the full qualified domain name to be verified.

changeDate.title: change_date
changeDate.description: ChangeDate is the timestamp of the verification of the Organization Domain.

userId.title: user_id
userId.description: optionally set your own id unique for the user.

username.title: username
username.description: optionally set a unique username, if none is provided the email will be used.

totpSecret.title: totp_secret
totpSecret.description:
An Implementation of RFC 6238 is used, with HMAC-SHA-1 and time-step of 30 seconds.
 Currently no other options are supported, and if anything different is used the validation will fail.

urlTemplate.title: url_template
urlTemplate.description:
Optionally set a url_template, which will be used in the verification mail sent by Zitadel to guide the user to your verification page.
 If no template is set, the default Zitadel url will be used.

 The following placeholders can be used: UserID, OrgID, Code

givenName.title: given_name
givenName.description: The given name is the first name of the user

familyName.title: family_name
familyName.description: The family name is the last name of the user

tags.name: zitadel.org.v2.OrganizationService
tags.description: This service is intended to manage organizations in a Zitadel instance.
